2606.05804.title: Can LLMs Be Constrained to the Past? Improving Knowledge Cutoff through Recall-Based Prompting
2606.05804.paper_summary: Prompted knowledge cutoff instructs a large language model (LLM) to act as if information beyond a specified cutoff date were unavailable. However, prior work mainly relies on direct-answer generation, which struggles when post-cutoff knowledge is not explicitly queried but is only causally related to the question. To address this limitation, we propose two recall-based prompting strategies: Self-Recall (SR), which asks the model to restate its cutoff constraint, and Question-Recall (QR), which requires the model to recall question-relevant information valid under the cutoff. Across three existing benchmarks, our methods outperform both direct-answer prompting and conventional step-by-step reasoning baselines, with particularly strong improvements on counterfactual questions. To investigate robustness across different cutoff settings, we further construct the Multi-cutoff Historical Event Benchmark (MHEB), which evaluates the same question under multiple cutoff years. Results show that knowledge cutoff performance varies with cutoff distance, while combining SR and QR consistently yields the best performance.
2606.05804.paper_summary_zh: 促使知識截止的指示要求大型語言模型（LLM）假裝在指定截止日期之後的信息不可用。然而，先前的研究主要依賴於直接答案生成，當截止後的知識未被明確詢問而僅與問題有因果關係時，這種方法會面臨困難。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了兩種基於回憶的提示策略：自我回憶（Self-Recall, SR），要求模型重述其截止約束；以及問題回憶（Question-Recall, QR），要求模型回憶在截止日期下與問題相關的有效信息。在三個現有基準中，我們的方法在直接答案提示和傳統逐步推理基準上均表現優於，尤其在反事實問題上有顯著改善。為了研究不同截止設置下的穩健性，我們進一步構建了多截止歷史事件基準（Multi-cutoff Historical Event Benchmark, MHEB），該基準在多個截止年份下評估相同問題。結果顯示，知識截止性能隨著截止距離的變化而變化，而結合SR和QR則始終產生最佳性能。
2606.05804.author: Michiro Asai et.al.
2606.05804.authors: Michiro Asai, Ailiang Lin, Yu Kishimoto, Takao Obi, Satoshi Kosugi, Kotaro Funakoshi, Manabu Okumura
